data.title: Developed market sovereign debt is no longer considered a safe haven due to increased governance risks; DoubleLine report shared in Tampa, FL.
data.description: In Tampa, Florida, as of December 23, 2025, the sovereign debt of developed market (DM) nations, historically considered secure from governance and societal risks seen in emerging markets (EM), is no longer guaranteed safe. The DoubleLine Global Bond Portfolio has highlighted this shift, signaling that DM countries are now vulnerable to potential financial instabilities.

data.title: Tigerlily Foundation introduces RACE Alliance, focusing on breast cancer education and care. Launch date: Dec. 23, 2025.
data.description: On December 23, 2025, Tigerlily Foundation unveiled the RACE ALLIANCE, a new initiative called the Rallying Around Community and Equity Alliance. This program aims to enhance understanding and knowledge of breast cancer education and care. The initiative is set to bring together a wide range of expertise and increase awareness on these important issues.

data.title: Santa Claus Rally favors certain stocks historically. S&P 500 rises an average 0.95% in the last trading week of Dec.
data.description: As we head into the final trading week of the year, the question on Wall Street is whether the Santa Claus Rally will make an appearance once again. Newmont stock is currently trading close to its recent highs, prompting traders to keep a close eye on NEM. Historical data suggests positive outcomes, with the U.S. stock market historically experiencing steady growth during the period from December 23 to December 31.According to Seasonax data, over 95 years of history, the S&P 500 has seen an average increase of 0.95% during the last trading week of the year. The index has ended this week higher in 69 instances, resulting in a 71% success rate. The most substantial Santa Claus Rally for the S&P 500 was observed in 2018 when it surged by 6.28% between December 24 and December 31.The Dow Jones Industrial Average has shown even stronger seasonal performance, with 128 years of data indicating an average gain of 1.06% in the final trading week of the year. The Dow has concluded this week on a positive note 92 times, leading to a 77% success rate. The Dow's most robust...For more information, refer to the complete article on Benzinga.com.

data.title: Banco Comercial Português, S.A. completes registration of share capital decrease and increase.
data.description: Portuguese commercial bank, Banco Comercial Português, S.A., has successfully completed the registration process for both a decrease and an increase in its share capital. This regulatory move will see the bank's financial structure adjust accordingly. The exact figures for the share capital changes have not been disclosed publicly.

data.title: AerCap delivers 1 of 10 new Airbus A321neo aircraft to Thai Airways. 9 more to follow for THAI.
data.description: AerCap Holdings N.V. ("AerCap" or the "Company") has recently completed the delivery of the initial Airbus A321neo aircraft out of a total order of ten to Thai Airways International Public Company Limited ("THAI"). The outstanding nine planes are set to be handed over to the airline in the near future. This transaction marks a significant milestone for AerCap as it strengthens its partnership with THAI.

data.title: Kosmera to unveil first prototype hypercar at CES 2026 in Las Vegas from Jan. 6-9, redefining intelligent mobility.
data.description: A company called Kosmera, focused on advancing intelligent mobility, revealed that their inaugural prototype, an energy-efficient hypercar, will premiere at the 2026 Consumer Electronics Show (CES) in Las Vegas from January 6–9.
BoC's Minutes: Governing council prepared to respond in case of a major new shock, or data showing economy and inflation diverging materially from the outlook.
BoC's Minutes: The governing council agreed to remain cautious in interpreting data, given recent volatility.
BoC Minutes: The governing council noted that despite the risk that higher costs from trade disruption could spill over into consumer prices, the effects have been limited so far.
BOC Minutes: Governing council concluded that this could be because of slower growth in unit labour costs.
BoC Minutes: Governing council felt uncertainty leading up to and during USMCA talks would likely weigh on business investment.
BoC's Minutes: Volatility in recent quarterly GDP readings underlines how challenging it will be to judge underlying economic trends.
BoC's Minutes: The governing council agreed that the upcoming review of the USMCA trade treaty was a significant risk.
